title: Hovering aerial vehicle and method of flying same
abstract: A hovering aerial vehicle is provided, comprising an airborne unit and an auxiliary unit. The airborne unit is configured to carry the auxiliary unit during flight and comprises a flight system configured to facilitate providing aerodynamic lift to facilitate hovering of the vehicle. The auxiliary unit comprises an electrical power source in electrical communication with the flight system to provide electrical power for its operation. The hovering aerial vehicle further comprises a decoupling system configured to facilitate selectively switching the vehicle between a detached configuration thereof wherein the airborne and auxiliary units are remote from each other, and an attached configuration thereof wherein the airborne and auxiliary units are secured to one another, and to maintain the electrical communication when the vehicle is in its detached configuration.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. A hovering aerial vehicle comprising an airborne unit and an auxiliary unit, wherein: â¢ said airborne unit is configured to carry said auxiliary unit during flight and comprises a flight system configured to facilitate providing aerodynamic lift to facilitate hovering of the vehicle; and â¢ said auxiliary unit comprises an electrical power source in electrical communication with said flight system to provide electrical power for its operation; said hovering aerial vehicle further comprising a decoupling system configured to: â¢ facilitate selectively switching said vehicle between a detached configuration thereof wherein said airborne and auxiliary units are remote from each other, and an attached configuration thereof wherein said airborne and auxiliary units are secured to one another; and â¢ maintain said electrical communication when the vehicle is in its detached configuration. 2. The vehicle according to claim 1, wherein the vehicle is configured, when in its attached configuration, to provide electrical communication between said electrical power source and said flight system using elements separate from said decoupling system. 3. The vehicle according to any one of claims 1 and 2, wherein said decoupling system comprises one or more cables configured to maintain said electrical communication and attached, at opposite ends thereof, to said airborne and auxiliary units. 4. The vehicle according to claim 3, said decoupling system further comprising a winch configured to selectively release and retract said cables. 5. The vehicle according to claim 4, wherein said winch is mounted to said airborne unit. 6. The vehicle according to claim 4, wherein said winch is mounted to said auxiliary unit. 7. The vehicle according to claim 3, said decoupling system comprising two of said cables, wherein points of attachments of said cables to the airborne unit are spaced from each other a first distance, and points of attachments of said cables to the 

 auxiliary unit are spaced from each other a second distance greater than said first distance. 8. The vehicle according to claim 7, wherein said decoupling system comprises a winch mounted to said airborne unit and configured to selectively release and retract said cables. 9. The vehicle according to any one of claims 7 and 8, being configured such that a first of said cables carries a positive electrical charge from said electrical power source to the flight system, and a second of said cables carries a negative electrical charge from said electrical power source to the flight system. 10. The vehicle according to any one of claims 3 through 9, wherein said decoupling system is configured to determine the tautness of the cables when the vehicle is in its detached configuration. 11. The vehicle according to claim 10, wherein said decoupling system comprises a motor and is configured to detect said tautness by measuring the current thereof. 12. The vehicle according to any one of claims 1 and 2, wherein said decoupling system is configured to maintain said electrical communication using wireless energy transmission. 13. The vehicle according to any one of the preceding claims, further comprising a tracking system configured to facilitate location of said auxiliary unit and to facilitate navigation of said airborne unit thereto. 14. The vehicle according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein said auxiliary unit comprises a floatation arrangement configured to facilitate the auxiliary arrangement floating on a body of water. 15. The vehicle according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein said auxiliary unit comprises a watertight housing containing said electrical power source there within. 16. The vehicle according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein said auxiliary unit comprises a camera. 17. The vehicle according to claim 16, wherein said camera is configured to transmit images captured thereby wirelessly. 18. The vehicle according to any one of claims 16 and 17, wherein said camera is configured to capture images as a video. 19. The vehicle according to any one of the preceding claims, being configured to facilitate recharging of said electrical power source. 20. The vehicle according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the weight of said auxiliary unit is at least 40% of the weight of the vehicle. 21. The vehicle according to claim 20, wherein the weight of said auxiliary unit is at least 50% of the weight of the vehicle. 22. The vehicle according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein said decoupling system is configured to determine a maximum separation distance between said airborne unit and said auxiliary unit within which it is configured to maintain said electrical communication in the detached configuration of the vehicle. 23. The vehicle according to claim 22, wherein said flight system is configured facilitate the airborne unit hovering, when the vehicle is in its detached configuration, above said auxiliary system up to a height equal to at least said maximum separation distance. 24. The vehicle according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein said flight system comprises one or more selected from the group including rotors, motors, and flight controllers. 25. The vehicle according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein said auxiliary unit further comprises a propulsion arrangement configured to facilitate locomotion thereof when the vehicle is in its detached configuration. 26. The vehicle according to claim 25, wherein said propulsion arrangement comprises a terrestrial propulsion arrangement configured to drive the auxiliary unit across land. 27. The vehicle according to claim 26, wherein said terrestrial propulsion arrangement comprises one or more terrestrial propulsion elements selected from the group including wheels, tracks, rollers, leg mechanisms, metachronal arrangements, and combinations thereof. 28. The vehicle according to any one of claims 25 through 27, wherein said propulsion arrangement comprises a marine propulsion arrangement configured to drive the auxiliary unit across water. 29. The vehicle according to claim 28, wherein said marine propulsion arrangement comprises one or more marine propulsion elements selected from the group including propellers, rudders, and combinations thereof. 30. The vehicle according to any one of claims 25 through 29, wherein said propulsion arrangement further comprises an auxiliary controller to direct operation thereof. 31. The vehicle according to claim 30, wherein said auxiliary controller is configured to communicate with said flight system of the airborne unit. 32. The vehicle according to any one of claims 25 through 31, wherein said propulsion arrangement is at least partially powered by said electrical power source. 33. The vehicle according to any one of claims 25 through 32, wherein said propulsion arrangement is at least partially powered by an auxiliary power source. 34. A method of flying a hovering aerial vehicle to deploy an auxiliary unit thereof, the method comprising: â¢ providing the vehicle according to any one of the preceding claims; â¢ said vehicle hovering, in its attached configuration, over a surface; â¢ releasing said auxiliary unit to rest on said surface; and â¢ said flight system providing aerodynamic lift, thereby facilitating ascension of the airborne unit from the auxiliary unit; wherein said aerodynamic lift is less than the weight of the vehicle, and greater than the weight of the airborne unit. 35. The method according to claim 34, wherein the aerodynamic lift exceeds the weight of the airborne unit by about 20% to about 50% of the weight of the auxiliary unit. 36. The method according to any one of claims 34 and 35, wherein said hovering of the vehicle is above the surface, and the releasing comprises lowering the auxiliary to rest on said surface. 37. A hovering aerial vehicle substantially as described herein and illustrated in the accompanying drawings. 38. A method of flying a hovering aerial vehicle substantially as described herein and illustrated in the accompanying drawings.
